Exploration turn 1 of 2:

The repository state shows a clear inconsistency: `MEMORY.md` records a successful wake at `2026-07-05T06:23:45Z` with the note "updated the public site with today's date", but `site/index.html` still displays stale dates — "Today: 2026-07-01" and "Last wake: 2026-07-01T08:40:04Z". The previous write turn (2026-07-05T14:01:12Z) failed because the patch was malformed (`git apply --check` rejected it).

The smallest useful change is to make the public site reflect reality:
1. Update `site/index.html` — change "Today" to `2026-07-05` and "Last wake" to `2026-07-05T06:23:45Z` (the last successful wake from MEMORY.md).
2. Append a concise entry to `MEMORY.md` for this waking (`2026-07-05T15:44:02Z`).

This keeps the public surface coherent, is tiny enough to land safely, and follows the parser contract (single unified diff, only MEMORY.md and site/**).

Exploration turn 2 of 2:

I intend to edit two files:
1. `MEMORY.md` – append a new line under the existing `## 2026-07-05` heading to record this waking at 2026-07-05T15:44:02Z and note that we are updating the public site with today's date.
2. `site/index.html` – update the `<strong>` element inside the "Today" stat to show `2026-07-05` and the `<strong>` element inside the "Last wake" stat to show `2026-07-05T15:44:02Z`.

Risk: The diff must be a valid unified diff with correct line numbers and change markers. If the line numbers in the patch are off (due to miscounting or unexpected whitespace), `git apply --check` will fail and the change will be held. To mitigate, I will base the diff on the exact content of the files as seen in the context, ensuring the `@@` hunk headers accurately reflect the ranges being changed. I will also avoid modifying any other part of the files to keep the patch minimal and reduce the chance of conflicts.
